agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians, with strict compliance controls around scheduling, cancellation, medical record access, and consultation note immutability.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled telemedicine consultation between exactly one patient and one licensed physician, with a defined start and end time."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A physician whose medical license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments overlap in time for the same physician. This is a forbidden state."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments within the 2-hour pre-appointment window."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician for a specific appointment. Becomes immutable (locked/read-only) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Patient Medical Records","definition":"Protected health information visible only to the patient themselves and the physician treating them in a given appointment."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's license number before the platform allows them to accept bookings."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before appointment start time, during which the patient retains self-service cancellation rights."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a particular appointment, and therefore granted access to that patient's medical records for that consultation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unlicensed practitioners to treat patients is a regulatory and patient-safety violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"No appointment record shall exist where the associated physician's license_verified status is false."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments whose time ranges overlap.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking violates the physical constraint that one physician can only conduct one consultation at a time. Allowing overlapping appointments corrupts scheduling integrity.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments with status != cancelled shall have overlapping [start_time, end_time) intervals."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request shall be rejected if current_time > (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment regardless of the cancellation window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational privilege policy. The threshold of who can override and under what conditions is a business decision, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a staff administrator shall succeed for any appointment that is not already cancelled, regardless of time remaining."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Patient medical records for a consultation are visible only to the patient and the treating physician of that appointment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a compliance and privacy invariant (likely HIPAA or equivalent). Unauthorized access constitutes a data breach — the system is in a violated state.","testable_assertion":"Any request to read a patient's medical records shall be denied unless the requesting user is the patient themselves or the physician assigned to a relevant appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Consultation notes become read-only 48 hours after the appointment ends.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Clinical record immutability is a regulatory/medico-legal requirement. Allowing edits after the lock window would compromise the legal integrity of the medical record.","testable_assertion":"Any write/update request to consultation notes shall be rejected if current_time > (appointment.end_time + 48 hours)."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before they can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a precondition for BR-1 enforced at the physician lifecycle level. An unverified physician appearing as bookable corrupts the trust model.","testable_assertion":"A physician shall not appear in booking search results or be selectable for appointments unless license_verified = true."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A patient is searching for available physicians","when":"The patient attempts to book an appointment with a physician whose license is not yet verified","then":"The system rejects the booking and informs the patient the physician is not currently available"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"Another patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The system rejects the booking with a scheduling conflict error"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:30 to 11:00","then":"The system accepts the booking because the intervals do not overlap (end-exclusive)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient has an appointment starting at 14:00 and the current time is 11:30","when":"The patient requests to cancel the appointment","then":"The cancellation succeeds because 11:30 is more than 2 hours before 14:00"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient has an appointment starting at 14:00 and the current time is 12:30","when":"The patient requests to cancel the appointment","then":"The cancellation is rejected because 12:30 is less than 2 hours before 14:00"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A patient has an appointment starting at 14:00 and the current time is 13:00","when":"A staff administrator requests to cancel the appointment","then":"The cancellation succeeds regardless of the cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient has a completed appointment with Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Jones (a different physician) attempts to access the patient's medical records for that consultation","then":"Access is denied because Dr. Jones is not the treating physician"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient has a completed appointment with Dr. Smith","when":"The patient themselves requests to view their medical records","then":"Access is granted"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An appointment ended at 09:00 on Monday and the current time is 08:00 on Wednesday (47 hours later)","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit succeeds because the 48-hour window has not yet elapsed"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment ended at 09:00 on Monday and the current time is 09:01 on Wednesday (48 hours and 1 minute later)","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected because the notes are now locked"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A patient has an appointment starting at 14:00 and the current time is exactly 12:00","when":"The patient requests to cancel the appointment","then":"The cancellation succeeds because the current time is exactly 2 hours before start (boundary: at least 2 hours)"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A physician has not yet had their license verified","when":"The physician's profile is displayed in search results","then":"The physician does not appear as bookable and no appointment slot is offered"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Patient attempts to cancel at exactly the 2-hour boundary","expected_behavior":"Cancellation succeeds. 'Up to 2 hours before' includes the exact 2-hour mark. The patient loses the right only when strictly less than 2 hours remain."},{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancelled by a staff administrator. The physician should be immediately removed from booking availability."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes edit attempted at exactly 48 hours after appointment end","expected_behavior":"At exactly 48 hours the notes lock. Edits at T+48h+0s should be rejected (the 48-hour window has elapsed)."},{"scenario":"Back-to-back appointments with zero gap for same physician","expected_behavior":"Allowed if using end-exclusive intervals (e.g., 10:00-10:30 and 10:30-11:00 do not overlap). The system must use consistent interval semantics."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to cancel an already-cancelled appointment","expected_behavior":"The system returns an error or no-op indicating the appointment is already cancelled. No duplicate cancellation event should be created."},{"scenario":"Patient has appointments with multiple physicians and requests record access","expected_behavior":"Each physician sees only records pertaining to their own appointment with the patient. Cross-physician record visibility is prohibited."},{"scenario":"Physician attempts to edit consultation notes for an appointment that was cancelled","expected_behavior":"Cancelled appointments should not have editable consultation notes. If notes exist, they follow the same 48-hour lock rule from the time of cancellation or original end time."},{"scenario":"Two concurrent booking requests for the same physician at overlapping times","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce the no-double-booking invariant atomically. One request succeeds, the other is rejected with a conflict error. Race conditions must not create overlapping appointments."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Booking compliance failures could result in regulatory fines (HIPAA violations can reach $1.9M+ per category). Cancellation policy protects physician revenue from last-minute no-shows.","operational":"Double-booking prevention ensures physician utilization is predictable and patients receive dedicated attention. License verification gates prevent liability exposure.","user":"Patients gain trust through transparent cancellation rules and confidence that their records are protected. Physicians benefit from clear scheduling and note-editing windows.","risk":"Primary risks are unauthorized medical record access (breach/lawsuit), unlicensed practitioners treating patients (regulatory action), and consultation note tampering after the immutability window (medico-legal exposure).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Directly affected by booking availability, cancellation rights, record privacy, and trust in physician licensing."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Schedule integrity, note-editing deadlines, and license verification gate their ability to practice on the platform."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Hold override authority for late cancellations; responsible for handling edge cases like license revocations."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Depend on record access controls and note immutability for regulatory audit readiness."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Bears liability for any invariant violation; reputation and regulatory standing at stake."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major domain rules without contradiction. Deducting confidence for a few areas where the spec is silent but real-world implementation would require clarification — these are noted as low-confidence areas but do not rise to the level of formal escalation since the core rules are unambiguous.","high_confidence_areas":["No-double-booking invariant is clearly specified","Cancellation policy with role-based override is well-defined","Medical record access control (patient + treating physician only)","Consultation note immutability at 48-hour mark","License verification as a precondition for bookings"],"low_confidence_areas":["Interval semantics (end-inclusive vs end-exclusive) for overlap detection — assumed end-exclusive as industry standard","Behavior when a physician's license is revoked after existing appointments are booked","Whether 'up to 2 hours' includes or excludes the exact 2-hour boundary — assumed inclusive (patient-favorable)","Whether the 48-hour lock is at exactly 48h or after 48h — assumed at exactly 48h","Handling of consultation notes for cancelled appointments","Whether 'treating physician' access extends beyond the specific appointment or grants ongoing access to the patient's full history"],"assumptions":["Appointment time intervals are half-open [start, end) for overlap calculation","The 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive (patient can cancel at exactly 2 hours before)","Consultation notes lock at exactly the 48-hour mark (not after)","'Treating physician' means the physician assigned to a specific appointment, not a long-term care relationship","Staff administrator cancellation has no time restriction whatsoever"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking:
Analyzing this telemedicine platform domain. Clear, well-specified rules. Let me produce the domain analysis.

No contradictions, no ambiguity worth escalating. Rules are clear. Let me classify invariants carefully.
